observations.statement: The staged contract documents POST /api/v2/download/count/ with a required filters object and an optional spending_level enum of subawards, transactions, or awards with default transactions.

The documented response fields are calculated_transaction_count, maximum_transaction_limit, transaction_rows_gt_limit, calculated_count, spending_level, maximum_limit, rows_gt_limit, and optional messages.

The AdvancedFilterObject section documents keyword, time period, geography, agency, recipient, award type, award ID, amount, program, NAICS, PSC, TAS, and treasury-account filter branches for this endpoint.

source.title: Shared search filter documentation for advanced filters

observations.statement: The shared search filter docs define Transaction Search Time Period objects with required start_date and end_date plus optional date_type values action_date, date_signed, last_modified_date, and new_awards_only.
The shared search filter docs define the StandardLocationObject, including country as required and state/county/district/zip constraints for domestic locations.
The shared search filter docs document that search start_date and end_date are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.

source.title: Current raw MCP profile for Download Count

observations.statement: The current raw MCP profile exposes filters and spending_level plus a subset of nested filter keys such as keywords, time_period, recipient_search_text, recipient_locations, recipient_type_names, award_type_codes, award_amounts, NAICS, PSC, TAS, treasury_account_components, and selected contract code filters.

The current raw profile omits documented filter branches including place_of_performance_scope, place_of_performance_locations, agencies, recipient_id, recipient_scope, award_ids, and program_numbers, and it does not expose time_period.date_type or the legacy subawards switch as discrete request facts.

The current raw profile records prior observations that an empty filters object returned HTTP 200 while missing filters produced a generic HTML 500, and that success responses included messages and row-limit flags.
The source view defines a legacy top-level subawards boolean with default false and a spending_level enum with default transactions.

The implementation computes one total_count and writes that same number into both calculated_transaction_count and calculated_count, with the same MAX_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT applied to both maximum_transaction_limit and maximum_limit.
Success responses always append two deprecation notices to the generic filters message list, so messages are always populated even before any unused-filter warning is added.

source.title: USAspending helper and integration tests for download count

observations.statement: The helper get_generic_filters_message always includes the time-period warning and adds an unused-filter warning when the original filters contain keys outside the allowed filter list.
Integration tests verify that a time_period object can include date_type=date_signed and that unknown filter keys surface in messages rather than nesting the warning under another object.
Integration tests show award-level counting when spending_level=awards and time-period-based transaction counting when spending_level=transactions.

source.title: Derived MCP coverage gap assessment for Download Count
source.locator: Derived from staged docs, current raw profile, and source implementation

observations.statement: Comparing the staged contract, shared filter docs, source, and current raw profile shows that the MCP surface needs explicit facts for subawards, time_period.date_type, place-of-performance fields, agencies, recipient_id, recipient_scope, award_ids, and program_numbers to avoid hiding valid request shapes from downstream agents.

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
'subawards' will be deprecated in the future. Set ‘spending_level’ to ‘subawards’ instead. See documentation for more information.
The above fields containing the transaction_* naming convention will be deprecated and replaced with fields without the transaction_*.

observations.statement: A baseline POST with only filters.time_period returned HTTP 200 and resolved spending_level to transactions.
The baseline response returned calculated_count=12868988, maximum_limit=500000, rows_gt_limit=true, and a populated messages array.
The live response included a warning that transaction_* fields will be deprecated and replaced by fields without the transaction_ prefix.

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
'subawards' will be deprecated in the future. Set ‘spending_level’ to ‘subawards’ instead. See documentation for more information.
The above fields containing the transaction_* naming convention will be deprecated and replaced with fields without the transaction_*.

observations.statement: The same filter set succeeded with spending_level=awards and returned HTTP 200.
The award-level response returned calculated_count=11507418 with rows_gt_limit=true and maximum_limit=500000.
In the award-level response, calculated_transaction_count again equaled calculated_count rather than surfacing a distinct transaction total.
An invalid spending_level value returned HTTP 400 with a structured JSON error naming the allowed values awards, transactions, and subawards.

observations.statement: A live request using filters.time_period.date_type=date_signed returned HTTP 200, confirming that the nested date_type field is supported for this endpoint.
The date_signed probe returned calculated_count=259770 with rows_gt_limit=false, demonstrating that the endpoint can return both over-limit and under-limit results depending on the filter slice.

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
The following filters from the request were not used: {'not_a_real_filter'}. See https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints for a list of appropriate filters
'subawards' will be deprecated in the future. Set ‘spending_level’ to ‘subawards’ instead. See documentation for more information.
The above fields containing the transaction_* naming convention will be deprecated and replaced with fields without the transaction_*.

observations.statement: A request containing an unknown nested filter key still returned HTTP 200 and included an unused-filter warning in messages.
The unused-filter probe produced a four-element messages array, confirming that messages content is variable and not limited to the three deprecation/time-period strings seen in the baseline probe.

source.title: Derived same-filter comparison of transactions versus awards responses
source.locator: Derived from live probes ev-live-baseline-default and ev-live-awards-same-filter

observations.statement: Using the same time_period filter, the transactions-default probe returned calculated_transaction_count=12868988 while the award-level probe returned calculated_transaction_count=11507418, and in both cases calculated_transaction_count equaled calculated_count. This contradicts the docs' description of calculated_transaction_count as an independent transaction total.

The same-filter transaction and award probes also showed maximum_transaction_limit=maximum_limit and transaction_rows_gt_limit=rows_gt_limit in both responses, reinforcing that the transaction_* fields currently behave as aliases.
